medicinal_plants_database.name: Neem
uses: Treats skin diseases like eczema and psoriasis
medicinal_plants_database.precautions: Avoid during pregnancy, may lower blood sugar excessively

medicinal_plants_database.name: Tulsi (Holy Basil)
uses: Treats coughs, colds, and respiratory infections
medicinal_plants_database.dosage: 5-7 leaves daily or tulsi tea twice a day
medicinal_plants_database.precautions: May affect blood clotting, consult doctor if on blood thinners

medicinal_plants_database.name: Kuppaimeni
medicinal_plants_database.precautions: Consult ayurvedic practitioner for proper dosage

medicinal_plants_database.name: Mint
medicinal_plants_database.precautions: Avoid excessive use if you have GERD

medicinal_plants_database.name: Curry Leaves
medicinal_plants_database.precautions: Generally safe, no major side effects

medicinal_plants_database.name: Moringa (Drumstick)
uses: Rich in vitamins A, C, and minerals

medicinal_plants_database.name: Aloe Vera
medicinal_plants_database.preparation: Fresh gel, juice, or topical application
medicinal_plants_database.precautions: Latex can cause diarrhea, use gel only

medicinal_plants_database.name: Papaya Leaves
medicinal_plants_database.precautions: Very bitter, may cause stomach upset

medicinal_plants_database.name: Betel Leaves
medicinal_plants_database.precautions: Avoid excessive chewing, may stain teeth

medicinal_plants_database.name: Ashwagandha
medicinal_plants_database.precautions: Avoid during pregnancy, may interact with thyroid medications

medicinal_plants_database.name: Ginger
medicinal_plants_database.precautions: May increase bleeding risk, avoid before surgery

medicinal_plants_database.name: Fenugreek
medicinal_plants_database.precautions: May cause uterine contractions, avoid during pregnancy

medicinal_plants_database.name: Coriander
medicinal_plants_database.dosage: Fresh leaves or 3-6 grams seeds daily

medicinal_plants_database.name: Eucalyptus
medicinal_plants_database.dosage: Few drops of oil for inhalation
medicinal_plants_database.precautions: Toxic if ingested in large amounts, use externally

medicinal_plants_database.name: Hibiscus
medicinal_plants_database.preparation: Flower tea, powder, or hair oil

medicinal_plants_database.name: Lemongrass
medicinal_plants_database.preparation: Tea, essential oil, or fresh leaves
medicinal_plants_database.precautions: Generally safe, avoid during pregnancy in large amounts

medicinal_plants_database.name: Amla (Indian Gooseberry)
uses: Rich in Vitamin C, boosts immunity
Improves hair health and prevents graying
medicinal_plants_database.preparation: Fresh fruit, juice, powder, or pickle
medicinal_plants_database.precautions: May increase bleeding risk in high doses

medicinal_plants_database.name: Tulsi Seeds (Sabja)
medicinal_plants_database.preparation: Soaked seeds in water or drinks
medicinal_plants_database.precautions: Children should avoid due to choking risk

medicinal_plants_database.name: Giloy (Guduchi)
medicinal_plants_database.precautions: May lower blood sugar, monitor levels

medicinal_plants_database.name: Arjuna
medicinal_plants_database.precautions: Consult doctor if on heart medications

medicinal_plants_database.name: Shatavari
medicinal_plants_database.preparation: Root powder, milk decoction, or capsules
medicinal_plants_database.precautions: May cause allergic reactions in some

medicinal_plants_database.name: Triphala (Combined)
medicinal_plants_database.scientific_name: Terminalia chebula, Terminalia bellirica, Emblica officinalis
medicinal_plants_database.preparation: Powder with warm water or honey

medicinal_plants_database.name: Karela (Bitter Gourd)
medicinal_plants_database.precautions: Very bitter, may lower blood sugar excessively

medicinal_plants_database.name: Ajwain (Carom Seeds)
medicinal_plants_database.precautions: Avoid during pregnancy in large amounts

medicinal_plants_database.name: Mulethi (Licorice)
medicinal_plants_database.precautions: Avoid long-term use, may increase blood pressure

medicinal_plants_database.name: Bhringraj
uses: Promotes hair growth and prevents graying

medicinal_plants_database.name: Haritaki
medicinal_plants_database.preparation: Powder with warm water or honey
medicinal_plants_database.precautions: May cause diarrhea in high doses

medicinal_plants_database.name: Vacha (Sweet Flag)
medicinal_plants_database.precautions: Use in small amounts, toxic in large doses

medicinal_plants_database.name: Gudmar
uses: Controls diabetes by reducing sugar absorption

medicinal_plants_database.name: Kaunch Beej (Velvet Bean)
medicinal_plants_database.precautions: Consult doctor if on Parkinson's medication

medicinal_plants_database.name: Maricha (Black Pepper)
medicinal_plants_database.precautions: May cause gastric irritation in excess

medicinal_plants_database.name: Shunthi (Dried Ginger)
medicinal_plants_database.preparation: Powder with warm water or honey

medicinal_plants_database.name: Manjistha
medicinal_plants_database.precautions: May cause reddish discoloration of urine

medicinal_plants_database.name: Kesar (Saffron)
medicinal_plants_database.preparation: Strands soaked in milk or water
medicinal_plants_database.precautions: Expensive, use sparingly, avoid during pregnancy in large amounts

medicinal_plants_database.name: Tejpatta (Bay Leaf)
medicinal_plants_database.preparation: Leaves in cooking, tea, or powder

medicinal_plants_database.name: Lavang (Clove)
medicinal_plants_database.dosage: 2-3 cloves daily or clove oil topically
medicinal_plants_database.precautions: Clove oil is very strong, dilute before use

medicinal_plants_database.name: Elaichi (Cardamom)
medicinal_plants_database.dosage: 2-3 pods daily in tea or food

medicinal_plants_database.name: Dalchini (Cinnamon)
medicinal_plants_database.precautions: Avoid in large amounts during pregnancy

medicinal_plants_database.name: Saunf (Fennel)
medicinal_plants_database.precautions: Generally safe, avoid large amounts during pregnancy

medicinal_plants_database.name: Kalonji (Black Seed)
medicinal_plants_database.precautions: May lower blood pressure, monitor levels

medicinal_plants_database.name: Sarso (Mustard)
medicinal_plants_database.precautions: May cause skin irritation if applied undiluted

medicinal_plants_database.name: Methi Patta (Fenugreek Leaves)
medicinal_plants_database.preparation: Fresh leaves as vegetable or dried powder
medicinal_plants_database.dosage: Handful of fresh leaves or 1-2 teaspoons dried

medicinal_plants_database.name: Palak (Spinach)
medicinal_plants_database.preparation: Fresh leaves as vegetable or juice
medicinal_plants_database.precautions: High in oxalates, limit if prone to kidney stones

medicinal_plants_database.name: Pudina (Mint)
medicinal_plants_database.precautions: Avoid excess if you have GERD

medicinal_plants_database.name: Lal Mirch (Red Chili)
medicinal_plants_database.precautions: May cause gastric irritation in excess

medicinal_plants_database.name: Hari Mirch (Green Chili)
medicinal_plants_database.precautions: May cause acidity if consumed in excess

medicinal_plants_database.name: Lahsun (Garlic)
medicinal_plants_database.precautions: May cause bad breath, interact with blood thinners

medicinal_plants_database.name: Pyaz (Onion)
medicinal_plants_database.precautions: May cause bad breath and gastric issues in some

medicinal_plants_database.name: Hara Dhania (Green Coriander)
medicinal_plants_database.preparation: Fresh leaves in food or juice

medicinal_plants_database.name: Kela Patta (Banana Leaf)
uses: Food served on leaf has health benefits
medicinal_plants_database.preparation: Fresh leaves for serving food or poultice
medicinal_plants_database.dosage: Used externally or as food plate

disclaimer: This information is for educational purposes only. Always consult with a qualified Ayurvedic practitioner or healthcare provider before using medicinal plants, especially if you are pregnant, nursing, taking medications, or have any health conditions. Dosages may vary based on individual constitution (Prakriti) and health condition.
